title: Hair problems | Hair loss | MedlinePlus
content: Hair Problems On this page Basics Summary Start Here Treatments and Therapies Learn More Related Issues Specifics Genetics See, Play and Learn Images Research Clinical Trials Resources Find an Expert For You Children Teenagers Women Patient Handouts Summary The average person has 5 million hairs. Hair grows all over your body except on your lips, palms, and the soles of your feet. It takes about a month for healthy hair to grow half an inch. Most hairs grow for up to six years and then fall out. New hairs grow in their place. Hair helps keep you warm. It also protects your eyes, ears and nose from small particles in the air. Common problem with the hair and scalp include hair loss , infections, and flaking .
